channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Nyne, founded by a father-son duo, gives AI agents the human context they’re missing
item.description: The data infrastructure startup raised $5.3 million in seed funding led by Wischoff Ventures and South Park Commons

item.title: Meta is bringing more international news to its AI
item.description: Meta AI should soon be better at surfacing international news content thanks to a set of new deals with publishers. The company announced new agreements with international outlets and offered additional details on its recent deal with News Corp. The latest deals bring French newspaper Le Figaro, Spanish media company Prisa and German newspaper Süddeutsche Zeitung into the fold. Together, along with News Corp, which runs a number of outlets in the UK, these sources should give Meta AI better access to timely info about world events. Meta didn't disclose terms of the deals — The Wall Street Jour...

item.title: A new 'work from home' age? Governments across Asia order employees to stay home due to concerns over the Iran war
item.description: Fuel shortages from the US-Israel war in Iran force governments across Asia to adopt remote work, energy conservation, and digital infrastructure strategies.

item.title: A Brief History of Vampires at the Oscars
item.description: 'Sinners' is the most nominated film of all time—so let’s prepare for its potential awards bloodbath by looking back at the fang-tastic nominees that came before.

item.title: Adobe agrees to pay settlement for making its subscriptions hard to cancel
item.description: Adobe has agreed to pay the US government $75 million to settle its lawsuit over the company's allegedly harmful approach to subscriptions. The suit started in 2024, when the US Department of Justice and the Federal Trade Commission filed a joint complaint alleging the company deliberately made it difficult to cancel subscriptions and obscured the frequently expensive "early termination fee" customers have to pay to get out of annual subscriptions that are paid monthly."While we disagree with the government’s claims and deny any wrongdoing, we are pleased to resolve this matter," Adobe writes....

item.title: Microsoft’s Copilot AI assistant is coming to current-gen Xbox consoles this year
item.description: Xbox is getting ready to launch its Gaming Copilot AI assistant on "current-generation consoles" this year, according to a report from GamesRadar. Sonali Yadav, Xbox's product manager for gaming AI, revealed the news during a panel at the Game Developers Conference (GDC), adding that the company will also add the assistant to "more services that […]...

item.title: Trump Mobile is just one in the crowd of conservative carriers
item.description: Where's the Trump phone? We're going to keep talking about it every week. This week, I wanted to see how Trump Mobile stacks up to its conservative carrier competition. Trump Mobile isn't unique. I mean, it is in some pretty specific senses - it's not every day the president's family launches a phone company while […]

item.title: Digg’s open beta shuts down after just two months, blaming AI bot spam
item.description: It's only been a year since Digg founder Kevin Rose, Reddit cofounder Alexis Ohanian, and a few others announced the link-sharing site would relaunch, promising a "social discovery built by communities, not by algorithms." Now, two months after opening its Reddit-like platform to the public, Digg is announcing a "hard reset" that's shutting down operations […]...

item.title: Facebook makes it easier for creators to report impersonators
item.description: Facebook is making it easier for creators to report impersonators while tightening rules around reposts and other unoriginal content.

item.title: Apple Studio Display XDR Review: It Looks So Good, I Wish It Were an iMac
item.description: Apple's replacement for the Pro Display XDR offers an amazing sound stage and still costs an enormous sum.

item.title: Rivian’s Highly Anticipated R2 Starts Sales in Spring With 330 Miles of Range
item.description: But it’ll be much more expensive than promised until next year.

item.title: Woodland Critter Spotted in Ohio Forest for the First Time in Over 150 Years
item.description: The fisher was driven out of many U.S. states by the 20th century due to overhunting and habitat loss.

item.title: Nothing updates its AI app with semantic search and a new way to track events
item.description: In the mad dash many companies have made to incorporate AI features into their phones, Nothing arrived at one of the better ideas with Essential Space on the Nothing Phone 3a in 2025. The AI-powered app turns screenshots and voice recordings into actionable to-do lists and transcriptions, and now Nothing is rolling out an update to make the app easier to search and capable of recognizing new kinds of content.As part of the update, Essential Space now recognizes "Events," displaying them in their own card with fields for the date, time and location. That means, for example, if you add a photo o...

item.title: ‘The Mandalorian and Grogu’ Might Bring Another EU Villain Back to Canon
item.description: Jonathan Favreau is teasing that the name of a particular mystery character will be known to fans—and given their role, there are some interesting candidates for who it might be.

item.title: The MacBook Neo is Apple's most repairable laptop
item.description: Apple's cheapest laptop is also its most repairable. iFixit gave the new MacBook Neo a 6/10 repairability score. Although that number would only be mediocre for, say, a game review or final exam grade, it's the MacBook line's highest iFixit score in about 14 years.As always, iFixit goes into great detail about the product's repairability, but a few points stand out. First, the MacBook Neo's battery is screwed down rather than glued — moving it from "this might burn the house down" to "routine repair" territory. The laptop also has a flat disassembly tree. That means its battery, speakers, port...

item.title: Amazon Is Jacking Up the Price on Prime Video Without Ads
item.description: The ad-free video streaming plan is getting more expensive, but some new perks are coming.

item.title: LG is adding support for Samsung's new Dolby Atmos rival to some of its TVs — though don't expect HDR10+ support to follow this new-found detente
item.description: Eclipsa Audio is coming to LG TVs, and there's a clear reason why this would be included when LG avoided similar alternatives.

item.title: Meta is killing end-to-end encryption in Instagram DMs
item.description: Meta is killing end-to-end encryption in Instagram DMs. The feature will "no longer be supported after May 8, 2026," the company wrote in an update on its support page. Unlike WhatsApp, Meta never made encryption available to all Instagram users and it was never a default setting. Instead, users in "some areas" had the ability to opt-in to encryption on a per-chat basis.In a statement, a Meta spokesperson said the feature was being retired due to low adoption. "Very few people were opting in to end-to-end encrypted messaging in DMs, so we're removing this option from Instagram in the coming mo...

item.title: Startup Emerges From Stealth With Bold Plan to Beam Solar Power to Satellites
item.description: Mantis Space will use $10 million in seed funding to develop its prototype payload.

item.title: Steven Spielberg says he’s ‘never used AI’ in any of his films
item.description: At SXSW, Steven Spielberg said AI has uses in many fields, but not when it comes to replacing creative people in film and TV writing.

item.title: More homeowners are considering solar power during the Iran oil crisis
item.description: EnergySage, an online marketplace, has seen a surge in people requesting quotes for solar installations since the conflict in the Middle East began. As the war in Iran shocks global gas and oil prices, more Americans are considering home solar and searching for EVs.

item.title: Instagram is getting rid of end-to-end encrypted DMs that ‘very few’ people used
item.description: Instagram will no longer support end-to-end encrypted messages starting May 8th. In a statement to The Verge, Meta spokesperson Dina El-Kassaby Luce says the platform is discontinuing the feature because "very few people" were using E2EE in their DMs. Instagram has begun notifying impacted users about the change inside its app, and has also posted […]...

item.title: The biggest AI stories of the year (so far)
item.description: The AI industry is constantly churning out news, like major acquisitions, indie developer successes, public outcry, and existentially dangerous contract negotiations.
